entries.definition:
كتاب القاف

قوم

(قوم) القاف والواو* والميم أصلانِ صحيحان، يدلُّ أحدهما على جماعةِ ناسٍ، وربِّما استعير في غيرهمْ. والآخَر على انتصابٍ أو عَزْم.

فالأوّل: القوم، يقولون: جمع امرئٍ، ولا يكون ذلك إلاّ للرِّجال. قال الله تعالى: ] لا يَسْخَرْ قَوْمٌ مِنْ قَوْمٍ [ [الحجرات 11]، ثمَّ قال: ] وَلاَ نِسَاءٌ مِنْ نِسَاءٍ [ [الحجرات 11].

وقال زُهير:

أقَوْمٌ آلُ حِصْنٍ أمْ نِسَاءُ

وما أدرِي وسَوْف إخالُ أدْري

ويقولون: قومٌ وأقوامٌ، وأقاوِمُ جمعُ جمعٍ. وأمَّا الاستعارة فقولُ القائل:

عِنْد الصَّباحِ وهو قومٌ مَعازيلُ

إذا أقْبَلَ الدِّيْكُ يَدعُو بَعْضَ أُسْرَتِهِ

فجمع وسَمَّاها قوماً.

وأمّا الآخر فقولُهم: قامَ قياماً، والقَوْمة المَرَّةُ الواحدة، إذا انتصب. ويكون قامَ بمعنى العَزيمة، كما يقال: قامَ بهذا الأمر، إذا اعتنَقَه. وهم يقولون في الأوَّل: قيامٌ حتم، وفي الآخر: قيامٌ عَزْم.

ومن الباب: قوَّمْتُ الشَّيء تقويماً. وأصل القِيمة الواو، وأصلُه أنَّك تُقيم هذا مكانَ ذاك.

وبلَغَنا أنَّ أهلَ مكّةَ يقولون: استَقَمْتُ المَتاعَ، أي قوَّمْتُه.

ومن الباب: هذا قِوام الدين والحقّ، أي به يقوم. وأمَّا القَوَام فالطُّول الحَسَن. والقُومِيَّة: القَوام والقامة. قال:

قيأ قيح قيد - قيل

* أيَّامَ كُنتُ حسَنَ القُومِيَّهْ *

(باب القاف والياء وما يثلثهما)

قَدْ يَعْلَمُ اللَّهُ الْمُعَوِّقِينَ مِنْكُمْ وَالْقَائِلِينَ لِإِخْوَانِهِمْ هَلُمَّ إِلَيْنَا وَلَا يَأْتُونَ الْبَأْسَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا God
may know [take to task] the hinderers among you, those who say
to their brothers, ‘Come join us,’ and they come to battle but little
II [n.] speaker (37:51) قَالَ قَائِلٌ مِنْهُمْ إِنِّي كَانَ لِي قَرِينٌ a speaker of them
said, ‘I had a close companion [on earth].’
entries.definition_english:
قَدْ يَعْلَمُ اللَّهُ الْمُعَوِّقِينَ مِنْكُمْ وَالْقَائِلِينَ لِإِخْوَانِهِمْ هَلُمَّ إِلَيْنَا وَلَا يَأْتُونَ الْبَأْسَ إِلَّا قَلِيلًا God
may know [take to task] the hinderers among you, those who say
to their brothers, ‘Come join us,’ and they come to battle but little
II [n.] speaker (37:51) قَالَ قَائِلٌ مِنْهُمْ إِنِّي كَانَ لِي قَرِينٌ a speaker of them
said, ‘I had a close companion [on earth].’

entries.definition:
q–w–m to stand, to halt, to stand up, to rise; to revolt, to resist,
to rebel; to erupt, outbreak; to set up, to reside, location, to be
constant; to be straight, to estimate, value, justice; backbone, to
support, pillar, substance, overseer, to guard over, guardian,
leader. ʾal-Suyūṭī suggests that the word قَيُّوم qayyūm might be a
borrowing from Syriac. Of this root, 25 forms occur 652 times in
the Qur’an: قَام qāma 33 times; أَقَام ʾaqāma 54 times; اِسْتَقَام
ʾistaqāma 10 times; قَائِم qāʾim eight times; قَائِمُون qāʾimūn twice;
قَائِمَة qāʾimatun five times; قِيَام qiyām seven times; قَوَّامُون
qawwāmūn three times; قَيُّوم qayyūm three times; أَقْوَم ʾaqwam four
times; مَقَام maqām 14 times; مُقَام muqām three times;
مُقَامَة muqāmatun once; مُقِيم muqīm eight times; مُقِيمِين muqīmīn
twice; قَيِّم qayyim five times; قَيِّمَة qayyimatun twice; قَوَام qawām
once; قِيَم qiyam once; إِقَام ʾiqām twice; إِقَامَة ʾiqāmatun once; تَقْوِيم
taqwīm once; مُسْتَقِيم mustaqīm 37 times; قِيَامَة qiyāmatun 70 times and
قَوْم qawm 382 times.

قَام qāma ū [v. intrans.] 1 to stand up (4:142) وَإِذَا قَامُوا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ
قَامُوا كُسَالَىٰ when they stand up to pray, they do so sluggishly 2 to
shoulder or undertake a difficult/great task, to assume [a big
responsibility] (74:1–2) يَاأَيُّهَا الْمُدَّثِّرُ قُمْ فَأَنْذِرْ you, wrapped up in your
cloak [and lying down], assume your responsibility and warn [all]
3 to rise from the dead (2:275) الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبَا لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِي
يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ الْمَسِّ those who take usury will [not] rise [on the
Day of Resurrection] except like one scourged by Satan’s touch 4
to be about to act, to undertake (5:6) يَاأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ
فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ إِلَى الْمَرَافِقِ you who believe, when you are about
to pray, wash your faces and your hands up to [also interpreted
as: including] the elbows 5 to stop, to halt, to stand still (2:20) كُلَّمَا
أَضَاءَ لَهُمْ مَشَوْا فِيهِ وَإِذَا أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ قَامُوا whenever it flashes on them they
walk on in it and when it darkens around them they stand still 6 to
stand in for, to be in the place of (5:107) فَآخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا two

783
entries.definition_english:
q–w–m to stand, to halt, to stand up, to rise; to revolt, to resist,

constant; to be straight, to estimate, value, justice; backbone, to
support, pillar, substance, overseer, to guard over, guardian,

قَائِمَة qāʾimatun five times; قِيَام qiyām seven times; قَوَّامُون
qawwāmūn three times; قَيُّوم qayyūm three times; أَقْوَم ʾaqwam four
times; مَقَام maqām 14 times; مُقَام muqām three times;
مُقَامَة muqāmatun once; مُقِيم muqīm eight times; مُقِيمِين muqīmīn
twice; قَيِّم qayyim five times; قَيِّمَة qayyimatun twice; قَوَام qawām
once; قِيَم qiyam once; إِقَام ʾiqām twice; إِقَامَة ʾiqāmatun once; تَقْوِيم

قَوْم qawm 382 times.

قَام qāma ū [v. intrans.] 1 to stand up (4:142) وَإِذَا قَامُوا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ
قَامُوا كُسَالَىٰ when they stand up to pray, they do so sluggishly 2 to
shoulder or undertake a difficult/great task, to assume [a big
responsibility] (74:1–2) يَاأَيُّهَا الْمُدَّثِّرُ قُمْ فَأَنْذِرْ you, wrapped up in your
cloak [and lying down], assume your responsibility and warn [all]
3 to rise from the dead (2:275) الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبَا لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِي
يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ الْمَسِّ those who take usury will [not] rise [on the
Day of Resurrection] except like one scourged by Satan’s touch 4
to be about to act, to undertake (5:6) يَاأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا قُمْتُمْ إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ
فَاغْسِلُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ إِلَى الْمَرَافِقِ you who believe, when you are about
to pray, wash your faces and your hands up to [also interpreted
as: including] the elbows 5 to stop, to halt, to stand still (2:20) كُلَّمَا
أَضَاءَ لَهُمْ مَشَوْا فِيهِ وَإِذَا أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ قَامُوا whenever it flashes on them they
walk on in it and when it darkens around them they stand still 6 to
stand in for, to be in the place of (5:107) فَآخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا two

783

entries.definition:
others [should] stand in for them 7 [with prep. عَلَى] to stand over
(9:84) وَلَا تُصَلِّ عَلَىٰ أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ مَاتَ أَبَدًا وَلَا تَقُمْ عَلَىٰ قَبْرِهِ and never pray
over any of them who die, and never stand over his grave 8 to
depart from, to rise from (27:39) قَالَ أَنَا آتِيكَ بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَقُومَ مِنْ مَقَامِكَ I will
bring it to you before you rise from your place 9 to worship, to
pray (9:108) لَا تَقُمْ فِيهِ أَبَدًا لَمَسْجِدٌ أُسِّسَ عَلَى التَّقْوَىٰ مِنْ أَوَّلِ يَوْمٍ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَقُومَ فِيهِ
never worship in it–a mosque founded from the first day on
consciousness of God is a more fitting place in which you
worship 10 to function, to exist (30:25) وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ أَنْ تَقُومَ السَّمَاءُ
وَالْأَرْضُ بِأَمْرِهِ and [one] of His wonders, is that the heavens and the
earth function by His command 11 to take place, to come to be
held (14:41) رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْحِسَابُ Lord, forgive
me, and my parents and the believers, on the Day when the
Reckoning is held 12 to discharge one’s duties, to undertake a
task, maintain/establish (4:127) وَأَنْ تَقُومُوا لِلْيَتَامَىٰ بِالْقِسْطِ and that you
discharge your duties towards the orphans in fairness 13 to
object to, to rebel (18:14) وَرَبَطْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ إِذْ قَامُوا فَقَالُوا رَبُّنَا رَبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ
وَالْأَرْضِ لَنْ نَدْعُوَا مِنْ دُونِهِ إِلَٰهًا لَقَدْ قُلْنَا إِذًا شَطَطًا We braced their hearts
when they rebelled and said, ‘Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens
and the earth; we will never call upon any god other than Him,
for that would be an outrageous thing to say.’

أَقَام ʾaqāma [v. IV, trans.] 1 to prop up, to erect, to support
(18:77) فَوَجَدَا فِيهَا جِدَارًا يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَنْقَضَّ فَأَقَامَهُ and they found a wall in it
that was on the point of collapsing [lit. wanting to collapse] so
then he set it up (or, supported it); *(30:30) فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفًا
devote yourself entirely [lit. set up your face] to the religion 2 to
observe, to maintain (9:11) فَإِنْ تَابُوا وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ if they turn to God
and maintain the prayer 3 to arrange, to organise, to set up, to
conduct (4:102) وَإِذَا كُنْتَ فِيهِمْ فَأَقَمْتَ لَهُمُ الصَّلَاةَ when you [Prophet] are
with them, and you conduct the prayer for them 4 to institute, to
keep up, to maintain (11:114) وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ طَرَفَيِ النَّهَارِ وَزُلَفًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ and
keep up the prayer at both ends of the day, and during parts of
the night 5 to abide by, to stand by, to uphold (5:66) وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ أَقَامُوا
التَّوْرَاةَ وَالْإِنْجِيلَ وَمَا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ if they had upheld the Torah and
the Gospel and what was sent down to them from their Lord 6 to
straighten, to establish, to perform in an upright manner (65:2)
وَأَقِيمُوا الشَّهَادَةَ لِلَّهِ and give witness [correctly] for the sake of God 7

784
entries.definition_english:
others [should] stand in for them 7 [with prep. عَلَى] to stand over
(9:84) وَلَا تُصَلِّ عَلَىٰ أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ مَاتَ أَبَدًا وَلَا تَقُمْ عَلَىٰ قَبْرِهِ and never pray
over any of them who die, and never stand over his grave 8 to
depart from, to rise from (27:39) قَالَ أَنَا آتِيكَ بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَقُومَ مِنْ مَقَامِكَ I will
bring it to you before you rise from your place 9 to worship, to
pray (9:108) لَا تَقُمْ فِيهِ أَبَدًا لَمَسْجِدٌ أُسِّسَ عَلَى التَّقْوَىٰ مِنْ أَوَّلِ يَوْمٍ أَحَقُّ أَنْ تَقُومَ فِيهِ
never worship in it–a mosque founded from the first day on

worship 10 to function, to exist (30:25) وَمِنْ آيَاتِهِ أَنْ تَقُومَ السَّمَاءُ
وَالْأَرْضُ بِأَمْرِهِ and [one] of His wonders, is that the heavens and the
earth function by His command 11 to take place, to come to be
held (14:41) رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيَّ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الْحِسَابُ Lord, forgive
me, and my parents and the believers, on the Day when the
Reckoning is held 12 to discharge one’s duties, to undertake a
task, maintain/establish (4:127) وَأَنْ تَقُومُوا لِلْيَتَامَىٰ بِالْقِسْطِ and that you
discharge your duties towards the orphans in fairness 13 to
object to, to rebel (18:14) وَرَبَطْنَا عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ إِذْ قَامُوا فَقَالُوا رَبُّنَا رَبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ
وَالْأَرْضِ لَنْ نَدْعُوَا مِنْ دُونِهِ إِلَٰهًا لَقَدْ قُلْنَا إِذًا شَطَطًا We braced their hearts
when they rebelled and said, ‘Our Lord is the Lord of the heavens
and the earth; we will never call upon any god other than Him,
for that would be an outrageous thing to say.’

أَقَام ʾaqāma [v. IV, trans.] 1 to prop up, to erect, to support
(18:77) فَوَجَدَا فِيهَا جِدَارًا يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَنْقَضَّ فَأَقَامَهُ and they found a wall in it
that was on the point of collapsing [lit. wanting to collapse] so
then he set it up (or, supported it); *(30:30) فَأَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفًا
devote yourself entirely [lit. set up your face] to the religion 2 to
observe, to maintain (9:11) فَإِنْ تَابُوا وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ if they turn to God
and maintain the prayer 3 to arrange, to organise, to set up, to
conduct (4:102) وَإِذَا كُنْتَ فِيهِمْ فَأَقَمْتَ لَهُمُ الصَّلَاةَ when you [Prophet] are
with them, and you conduct the prayer for them 4 to institute, to
keep up, to maintain (11:114) وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ طَرَفَيِ النَّهَارِ وَزُلَفًا مِنَ اللَّيْلِ and
keep up the prayer at both ends of the day, and during parts of
the night 5 to abide by, to stand by, to uphold (5:66) وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ أَقَامُوا
التَّوْرَاةَ وَالْإِنْجِيلَ وَمَا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ if they had upheld the Torah and
the Gospel and what was sent down to them from their Lord 6 to
straighten, to establish, to perform in an upright manner (65:2)
وَأَقِيمُوا الشَّهَادَةَ لِلَّهِ and give witness [correctly] for the sake of God 7

784

entries.definition:
to assign to (18:105) فَلَا نُقِيمُ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَزْنًا We will not hold any
weighing for them [their deeds], on the Day of Resurrection (or,
We shall not assign any weight to them on the Day of
Resurrection).

اِسْتَقَام ʾistaqāma [v. X, intrans.] to go straight, to become
upright, to follow the correct path (46:13) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ
اسْتَقَامُوا those who say, ‘Our lord is God,’ and then become
upright; *(9:7) فَمَا اسْتَقَامُوا لَكُمْ فَاسْتَقِيمُوا لَهُمْ so long as they remain true
to you be true to them.

قَائِم qāʾim I [act. part.; pl. قَائِمُون qāʾimūn] 1 one standing (3:39)
فَنَادَتْهُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ وَهُوَ قَائِمٌ يُصَلِّي فِي الْمِحْرَابِ so the angels called out to him,
while he stood praying in the sanctuary 2 watching, observing
(13:33) أَفَمَنْ هُوَ قَائِمٌ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ نَفْسٍ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ is He who is aware of the
actions of every soul [as he who is aware of nothing?] 3 one
being constant at (3:75) وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ إِنْ تَأْمَنْهُ بِدِينَارٍ لَا يُؤَدِّهِ إِلَيْكَ إِلَّا مَا دُمْتَ عَلَيْهِ
قَائِمًا but of them is he who, if you trust him [over] with a [single]
dinar, will not return it to you, unless you persist in standing over
him 4 that which is taking place, coming true (18:36) وَمَا أَظُنُّ السَّاعَةَ
قَائِمَةً I do not think the Hour will take place 5 supporting,
upholding (3:18) شَهِدَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ وَأُولُو الْعِلْمِ قَائِمًا بِالْقِسْطِ
God bears witness, that there is no god but He, as do the angels
and those imbued with knowledge, upholding justice II [quasi-act.
part.] upright (3:113) مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ أُمَّةٌ قَائِمَةٌ يَتْلُونَ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ آنَاءَ اللَّيْلِ
among the People of the Book there is an upright group who
recite God’s revelations throughout the night.

قِيَام qiyām I [v. n.] 1 rising, standing up (51:45) فَمَا اسْتَطَاعُوا مِنْ قِيَامٍ
وَمَا كَانُوا مُنْتَصِرِينَ they could not manage to stand and never were
they able to defend themselves 2 supporting, providing security, a
standard (5:97) جَعَلَ اللَّهُ الْكَعْبَةَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ قِيَامًا لِلنَّاسِ God has made the
Kaʿba–the Sacred House–a standard for humankind II [v. n.; n.]
support, mainstay, livelihood (4:5) وَلَا تُؤْتُوا السُّفَهَاءَ أَمْوَالَكُمُ الَّتِي جَعَلَ اللَّهُ
لَكُمْ قِيَامًا and do not entrust to the feeble-minded your property (or,
the properties of the feeble-minded) over which God has assigned
to you responsibility III [pl. of act. part. قَائِم qāʾim] those who are
standing up, those who are rising up (39:68) ثُمَّ نُفِخَ فِيهِ أُخْرَىٰ فَإِذَا هُمْ
قِيَامٌ يَنْظُرُونَ then it is blown another [time] and, behold, they are

785
entries.definition_english:
to assign to (18:105) فَلَا نُقِيمُ لَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَزْنًا We will not hold any
weighing for them [their deeds], on the Day of Resurrection (or,
We shall not assign any weight to them on the Day of
Resurrection).

اِسْتَقَام ʾistaqāma [v. X, intrans.] to go straight, to become
upright, to follow the correct path (46:13) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ
اسْتَقَامُوا those who say, ‘Our lord is God,’ and then become
upright; *(9:7) فَمَا اسْتَقَامُوا لَكُمْ فَاسْتَقِيمُوا لَهُمْ so long as they remain true
to you be true to them.

قَائِم qāʾim I [act. part.; pl. قَائِمُون qāʾimūn] 1 one standing (3:39)
فَنَادَتْهُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ وَهُوَ قَائِمٌ يُصَلِّي فِي الْمِحْرَابِ so the angels called out to him,
while he stood praying in the sanctuary 2 watching, observing
(13:33) أَفَمَنْ هُوَ قَائِمٌ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ نَفْسٍ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ is He who is aware of the
actions of every soul [as he who is aware of nothing?] 3 one
being constant at (3:75) وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ إِنْ تَأْمَنْهُ بِدِينَارٍ لَا يُؤَدِّهِ إِلَيْكَ إِلَّا مَا دُمْتَ عَلَيْهِ
قَائِمًا but of them is he who, if you trust him [over] with a [single]
dinar, will not return it to you, unless you persist in standing over
him 4 that which is taking place, coming true (18:36) وَمَا أَظُنُّ السَّاعَةَ
قَائِمَةً I do not think the Hour will take place 5 supporting,
upholding (3:18) شَهِدَ اللَّهُ أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ وَأُولُو الْعِلْمِ قَائِمًا بِالْقِسْطِ
God bears witness, that there is no god but He, as do the angels
and those imbued with knowledge, upholding justice II [quasi-act.
part.] upright (3:113) مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ أُمَّةٌ قَائِمَةٌ يَتْلُونَ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ آنَاءَ اللَّيْلِ
among the People of the Book there is an upright group who
recite God’s revelations throughout the night.

قِيَام qiyām I [v. n.] 1 rising, standing up (51:45) فَمَا اسْتَطَاعُوا مِنْ قِيَامٍ
وَمَا كَانُوا مُنْتَصِرِينَ they could not manage to stand and never were
they able to defend themselves 2 supporting, providing security, a
standard (5:97) جَعَلَ اللَّهُ الْكَعْبَةَ الْبَيْتَ الْحَرَامَ قِيَامًا لِلنَّاسِ God has made the
Kaʿba–the Sacred House–a standard for humankind II [v. n.; n.]
support, mainstay, livelihood (4:5) وَلَا تُؤْتُوا السُّفَهَاءَ أَمْوَالَكُمُ الَّتِي جَعَلَ اللَّهُ
لَكُمْ قِيَامًا and do not entrust to the feeble-minded your property (or,
the properties of the feeble-minded) over which God has assigned
to you responsibility III [pl. of act. part. قَائِم qāʾim] those who are
standing up, those who are rising up (39:68) ثُمَّ نُفِخَ فِيهِ أُخْرَىٰ فَإِذَا هُمْ
قِيَامٌ يَنْظُرُونَ then it is blown another [time] and, behold, they are

785

entries.definition:
standing, looking on.
قَوَّامُون qawwāmūn [pl. of intens. act. part. قَوَّام qawwām] 1 those
who are most upright, those who are steadfast, guardians (4:135)
كُونُوا قَوَّامِينَ بِالْقِسْطِ شُهَدَاءَ لِلَّهِ be upholders of justice and bear witness
for God 2 those who are responsible for, take care of, in charge of
(4:34) الرِّجَالُ قَوَّامُونَ عَلَى النِّسَاءِ بِمَا فَضَّلَ اللَّهُ بَعْضَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ وَبِمَا أَنْفَقُوا مِنْ
أَمْوَالِهِمْ men are in charge of women by virtue of what God has
bestowed on some of them in preference to others and by the
spending of their money [on women].

قَيُّوم qayyūm [intens. act. part.] constant, eternal, guardian,
benefactor *(2:255) القيوم [an attribute of God] the Supporter [of
all], the Constant, the Everlasting, the Independent.

أَقْوَم ʾaqwam [elat.] 1 more/most upright (2:282) ذَٰلِكُمْ أَقْسَطُ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ
وَأَقْوَمُ لِلشَّهَادَةِ this way is more equitable in God’s eyes, and more
reliable as testimony 2 more/most precise (73:6) إِنَّ نَاشِئَةَ اللَّيْلِ هِيَ أَشَدُّ
وَطْئًا وَأَقْوَمُ قِيلًا night worshipping is most taxing [on those who
conduct it] (or, makes a deeper impression) but far reaching in
prayer [lit. speech].

مَقَام maqām I [v. n.] staying (10:71) إِنْ كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكُمْ مَقَامِي وَتَذْكِيرِي
بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ if my staying among you and my reminding [you] of
God’s signs are burdensome on you II [n. of place] 1 physical
place, location (2:125) وَاتَّخِذُوا مِنْ مَقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ مُصَلًّى and take the spot
where Abraham stood as a place of prayer 2 seat, seating place
(27:39) أَنَا آتِيكَ بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَقُومَ مِنْ مَقَامِكَ I will bring it to you before
you rise from your seat 3 rank, position, status (37:164) وَمَا مِنَّا إِلَّا لَهُ
مَقَامٌ مَعْلُومٌ there is not a single one of us but has a known rank;
*(5:107) فَآخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا two others [should] replace them (or,
stand in their place) 4 situation, abode, dwelling (26:58) وَكُنُوزٍ وَمَقَامٍ
كَرِيمٍ treasures and a noble dwelling.

مُقَام muqām [n. of place] 1 place; stand (33:13) يَاأَهْلَ يَثْرِبَ لَا مُقَامَ
لَكُمْ فَارْجِعُوا people of Yathrib, there is no place for you [among the
defending army], so go back! 2 abode, position (25:76) خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا
حَسُنَتْ مُسْتَقَرًّا وَمُقَامًا abiding there forever–fair it is as a station and
abode.

مُقَامَة muqāmatun [n. of place; v. n.] place, abode of

786
entries.definition_english:
standing, looking on.
قَوَّامُون qawwāmūn [pl. of intens. act. part. قَوَّام qawwām] 1 those
who are most upright, those who are steadfast, guardians (4:135)
كُونُوا قَوَّامِينَ بِالْقِسْطِ شُهَدَاءَ لِلَّهِ be upholders of justice and bear witness
for God 2 those who are responsible for, take care of, in charge of
(4:34) الرِّجَالُ قَوَّامُونَ عَلَى النِّسَاءِ بِمَا فَضَّلَ اللَّهُ بَعْضَهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ وَبِمَا أَنْفَقُوا مِنْ
أَمْوَالِهِمْ men are in charge of women by virtue of what God has
bestowed on some of them in preference to others and by the
spending of their money [on women].

قَيُّوم qayyūm [intens. act. part.] constant, eternal, guardian,
benefactor *(2:255) القيوم [an attribute of God] the Supporter [of
all], the Constant, the Everlasting, the Independent.

أَقْوَم ʾaqwam [elat.] 1 more/most upright (2:282) ذَٰلِكُمْ أَقْسَطُ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ
وَأَقْوَمُ لِلشَّهَادَةِ this way is more equitable in God’s eyes, and more
reliable as testimony 2 more/most precise (73:6) إِنَّ نَاشِئَةَ اللَّيْلِ هِيَ أَشَدُّ
وَطْئًا وَأَقْوَمُ قِيلًا night worshipping is most taxing [on those who
conduct it] (or, makes a deeper impression) but far reaching in
prayer [lit. speech].

مَقَام maqām I [v. n.] staying (10:71) إِنْ كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكُمْ مَقَامِي وَتَذْكِيرِي
بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ if my staying among you and my reminding [you] of
God’s signs are burdensome on you II [n. of place] 1 physical
place, location (2:125) وَاتَّخِذُوا مِنْ مَقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ مُصَلًّى and take the spot
where Abraham stood as a place of prayer 2 seat, seating place
(27:39) أَنَا آتِيكَ بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَقُومَ مِنْ مَقَامِكَ I will bring it to you before
you rise from your seat 3 rank, position, status (37:164) وَمَا مِنَّا إِلَّا لَهُ
مَقَامٌ مَعْلُومٌ there is not a single one of us but has a known rank;
*(5:107) فَآخَرَانِ يَقُومَانِ مَقَامَهُمَا two others [should] replace them (or,
stand in their place) 4 situation, abode, dwelling (26:58) وَكُنُوزٍ وَمَقَامٍ
كَرِيمٍ treasures and a noble dwelling.

مُقَام muqām [n. of place] 1 place; stand (33:13) يَاأَهْلَ يَثْرِبَ لَا مُقَامَ
لَكُمْ فَارْجِعُوا people of Yathrib, there is no place for you [among the
defending army], so go back! 2 abode, position (25:76) خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا
حَسُنَتْ مُسْتَقَرًّا وَمُقَامًا abiding there forever–fair it is as a station and
abode.

مُقَامَة muqāmatun [n. of place; v. n.] place, abode of

786

entries.definition:
permanence; act of staying (35:35) الَّذِي أَحَلَّنَا دَارَ الْمُقَامَةِ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ He
who has, from His bounty, settled us in the home of permanence.

مُقِيم muqīm [quasi-act. part; pl. مُقِيمُون muqīmūn] 1 one who
is/that which is lasting, constant (9:21) لَهُمْ فِيهَا نَعِيمٌ مُقِيمٌ in which
they will have lasting bliss 2 one who observes, maintains,
upholds (14:40) رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةِ my Lord, make me constant in
performing the prayer 3 that which is straight; lasting (15:76) وَإِنَّهَا
لَبِسَبِيلٍ مُقِيمٍ indeed, they [the towns of the people of Lot and Midian]
are on a highway [till now] remaining.

قَيِّم qayyim [quasi-act. part.] straight, true (12:40) ذَٰلِكَ الدِّينُ الْقَيِّمُ
that is the true faith.

قَوَامًا qawāman [n.] straightness, correct balance (25:67) وَالَّذِينَ إِذَا
أَنْفَقُوا لَمْ يُسْرِفُوا وَلَمْ يَقْتُرُوا وَكَانَ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ قَوَامًا and there are those who,
when they spend, they neither waste nor are stingy, but keep
between these to a just balance.

قِيَم qiyam [quasi-act. part.] true, straight, upright (6:161) إِنَّنِي
هَدَانِي رَبِّي إِلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ دِينًا قِيَمًا my Lord has guided me to a
straight path, an upright religion.

إِقَام ʾiqām (= إِقَامَة ʾiqāmatun) [v. n. of v. IV, trans. (with the
final ة elided as means of directing attention to the context in
which the word appears)] act of observing, maintaining,
upholding (21:73) وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ فِعْلَ الْخَيْرَاتِ وَإِقَامَ الصَّلَاةِ وَإِيتَاءَ الزَّكَاةِ and
We inspired in them the doing of good, the upholding of prayer
and the giving of alms.

إِقَامَة ʾiqāmatun [v. n. of v. IV] act of staying (16:80) وَجَعَلَ لَكُمْ مِنْ
جُلُودِ الْأَنْعَامِ بُيُوتًا تَسْتَخِفُّونَهَا يَوْمَ ظَعْنِكُمْ وَيَوْمَ إِقَامَتِكُمْ and He provided for you,
from the hides of cattle, [houses] tents [that] you find light on the
day you travel and on the day you settle down.

تَقْوِيم taqwīm [v. n. of v. II; n.] forming, moulding; stature
(95:4) لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسَانَ فِي أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍ and indeed, We fashioned
humankind in the best of statures.

مُسْتَقِيم mustaqīm [quasi-act. part.] straight, upright, righteous
(46:30) يَهْدِي إِلَى الْحَقِّ وَإِلَىٰ طَرِيقٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ guiding to the truth and to a
straight path.

787
entries.definition_english:
permanence; act of staying (35:35) الَّذِي أَحَلَّنَا دَارَ الْمُقَامَةِ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ He
who has, from His bounty, settled us in the home of permanence.

final ة elided as means of directing attention to the context in
which the word appears)] act of observing, maintaining,
upholding (21:73) وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَيْهِمْ فِعْلَ الْخَيْرَاتِ وَإِقَامَ الصَّلَاةِ وَإِيتَاءَ الزَّكَاةِ and
We inspired in them the doing of good, the upholding of prayer
and the giving of alms.

جُلُودِ الْأَنْعَامِ بُيُوتًا تَسْتَخِفُّونَهَا يَوْمَ ظَعْنِكُمْ وَيَوْمَ إِقَامَتِكُمْ and He provided for you,
from the hides of cattle, [houses] tents [that] you find light on the
day you travel and on the day you settle down.

تَقْوِيم taqwīm [v. n. of v. II; n.] forming, moulding; stature
(95:4) لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسَانَ فِي أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍ and indeed, We fashioned
humankind in the best of statures.

مُسْتَقِيم mustaqīm [quasi-act. part.] straight, upright, righteous
(46:30) يَهْدِي إِلَى الْحَقِّ وَإِلَىٰ طَرِيقٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ guiding to the truth and to a
straight path.

787
